div1.entryFree:
Fling, vb. (impf. and part. flung) 1) trans. to throw: the water, whose enmity he flung aside, Tp. II, 1, 116. here I'll f. the pillow, Shr. IV, 1, 204. the mouth of passage shall we f. wide ope, John II, 449. f. up his cap, H6B IV, 8, 15. f. it at thy face, H6C V, 1, 51. which (accusations) he fain would have flung from him, H8 II, 1, 25. f. away ambition, III, 2, 441. matrons flung gloves upon him, Cor. II, 1, 279.
2) intr. to rush: he's flung in rage from this ingrateful seat of monstrous friends, Tim. IV, 2, 45. Duncan's horses broke their stalls, flung out, Mcb. II, 4, 16 (or is it, by a hysteron-proteron, == kicked out?).
